messages.content: Oscar Academy Awards 2012 Winners and Nominees for Best Original Song.

The 84th Academy Awards, honoring the best in film for 2011, were held on February 26, 2012. The nominees for Best Original Song were:

1. "Man or Muppet" from "The Muppets" – Music and Lyric by Bret McKenzie
2. "Real in Rio" from "Rio" – Music by Sérgio Mendes and Carlinhos Brown, Lyric by Siedah Garrett

And the winner of the Best Original Song Oscar for the 2012 Academy Awards was "Man or Muppet" from "The Muppets", composed by Bret McKenzie.
Oscar Academy Awards 2011 Winners and Nominees for Best Original Song.

The 83rd Academy Awards, which took place on February 27, 2011, recognized films from 2010. Here is the list of the nominees and winner for the Best Original Song category:

1. "Coming Home" from "Country Strong" – Music and Lyric by Tom Douglas, Troy Verges, and Hillary Lindsey
2. "I See the Light" from "Tangled" – Music by Alan Menken, Lyric by Glenn Slater
3. "If I Rise" from "127 Hours" – Music by A.R. Rahman, Lyric by Dido and Rollo Armstrong
4. "We Belong Together" from "Toy Story 3" – Music and Lyric by Randy Newman

The winner of the Best Original Song Oscar for the 2011 Academy Awards was "We Belong Together" from "Toy Story 3", composed by Randy Newman.
Oscar Academy Awards 2014 Winners and Nominees for Best Original Song.

The 86th Academy Awards, which took place on March 2, 2014, honored the films of 2013. The nominees and winner for the Best Original Song category were:

1. "Happy" from "Despicable Me 2" – Music and Lyric by Pharrell Williams
2. "Let It Go" from "Frozen" – Music and Lyric by Kristen Anderson-Lopez and Robert Lopez
3. "The Moon Song" from "Her" – Music by Karen O; Lyric by Karen O and Spike Jonze
4. "Ordinary Love" from "Mandela: Long Walk to Freedom" – Music by Paul Hewson, Dave Evans, Adam Clayton, and Larry Mullen (U2); Lyric by Paul Hewson

The winner of the Best Original Song Oscar for the 2014 Academy Awards was "Let It Go" from "Frozen", composed by Kristen Anderson-Lopez and Robert Lopez.
Oscar Academy Awards 2014 Winners and Nominees for Best Animated Feature Film

The 86th Academy Awards presented the Best Animated Feature Film award to the following nominees, with the winner listed first:

1. "Frozen" – Chris Buck, Jennifer Lee, and Peter Del Vecho
2. "The Croods" – Kirk DeMicco, Chris Sanders, and Kristine Belson
3. "Despicable Me 2" – Chris Renaud, Pierre Coffin, and Chris Meledandri
4. "Ernest & Celestine" – Benjamin Renner and Didier Brunner
5. "The Wind Rises" – Hayao Miyazaki and Toshio Suzuki

The winner of the Best Animated Feature Film for the 2014 Academy Awards was "Frozen".
Golden Globe 2014 Winners and Nominees for Best Animated Feature Film

For the 2014 Golden Globe Awards, which took place on January 12, 2014, honoring films from 2013, the nominees for the Best Animated Feature Film category were:

1. "Frozen" – Walt Disney Animation Studios
2. "The Croods" – DreamWorks Animation
3. "Despicable Me 2" – Illumination Entertainment

The winner of the Best Animated Feature Film at the 2014 Golden Globes was "Frozen" from Walt Disney Animation Studios.
Golden Globe 2023 Winners and Nominees for Best Animated Feature Film

As of my last update in April 2023, the 80th Golden Globe Awards took place on January 10, 2023. Below are the nominees and the winner for the Best Animated Feature Film of that year:

1. "Guillermo del Toro's Pinocchio" – Guillermo del Toro, Mark Gustafson, Gary Ungar, Alex Bulkley, Corey Campodonico, and Lisa Henson
2. "Inu-Oh" – Masaaki Yuasa and Fumie Takeuchi
3. "Marcel the Shell with Shoes On" – Dean Fleischer Camp, Elisabeth Holm, Andrew Goldman, Caroline Kaplan, and Paul Mezey
4. "Puss in Boots: The Last Wish" – Joel Crawford and Mark Swift
5. "Turning Red" – Domee Shi and <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>

The winner of the Best Animated Feature Film at the 2023 Golden Globes was "Guillermo del Toro's Pinocchio".
Oscar Academy Awards 2023 Winners and Nominees for Best Animated Feature Film

As of my last update in April 2023, I do not have access to current real-time data, and therefore, I cannot provide you with the winners and nominees for the Oscar Academy Awards in 2023 for the Best Animated Feature Film category or any other category.

For the most accurate and up-to-date information, please check the latest sources such as the official Oscars website, major entertainment news outlets, or reliable media coverage of the event.
Golden Globe 2011 Winners and Nominees for Best Animated Feature Film

The Golden Globe Awards for 2011 honored films released in 2010. Here are the nominees for the Best Animated Feature Film category, with the winner highlighted:

1. "Toy Story 3" – Walt Disney Pictures/Pixar Animation Studios; Walt Disney Studios Motion Pictures (Winner)
2. "Despicable Me" – Illumination Entertainment; Universal Pictures
3. "How to Train Your Dragon" – DreamWorks Animation; Paramount Pictures
4. "The Illusionist" – Django Films; Sony Pictures Classics
5. "Tangled" – Walt Disney Animation Studios; Walt Disney Studios Motion Pictures

"Toy Story 3" was the victor at the 2011 Golden Globe Awards for Best Animated Feature Film.
Golden Globe 2013 Winners and Nominees for Best Animated Feature Film

The 70th Golden Globe Awards honored films from 2012, and the nominees for the Best Animated Feature Film category were:

1. "Brave" – Pixar Animation Studios; Walt Disney Pictures
2. "Frankenweenie" – Tim Burton Productions; Walt Disney Pictures
3. "Hotel Transylvania" – Sony Pictures Animation; Columbia Pictures
4. "Rise of the Guardians" – DreamWorks Animation; Paramount Pictures
5. "Wreck-It Ralph" – Walt Disney Animation Studios; Walt Disney Pictures

The winner of the Best Animated Feature Film at the 2013 Golden Globes was "Brave" from Pixar Animation Studios.
Golden Globe 2020  Winners and Nominees for Best Animated Feature Film

The 77th Golden Globe Awards, which took place in 2020, recognized films released in 2019. The nominees for Best Animated Feature Film were:

1. "Frozen II" – Walt Disney Animation Studios
2. "How to Train Your Dragon: The Hidden World" – DreamWorks Animation
3. "The Lion King" – Walt Disney Pictures
4. "Missing Link" – Laika
5. "Toy Story 4" – Pixar Animation Studios

The winner of the Golden Globe for Best Animated Feature Film in 2020 was "Missing Link" produced by Laika.
Oscar Academy Awards 2020 Winners and Nominees for Best Animated Feature Film

The 92nd Academy Awards, which took place in 2020, honored the best films of 2019. Here are the nominees and winner for the Best Animated Feature Film category:

1. "How to Train Your Dragon: The Hidden World" – Dean DeBlois, Bradford Lewis, and Bonnie Arnold
2. "I Lost My Body" – Jérémy Clapin and Marc du Pontavice
3. "Klaus" – Sergio Pablos, Jinko Gotoh, and Marisa Román
4. "Missing Link" – Chris Butler, Arianne Sutner, and Travis Knight
5. "Toy Story 4" – Josh Cooley, Mark Nielsen, and Jonas Rivera

The winner of the Best Animated Feature Film at the 2020 Academy Awards was "Toy Story 4".
Annie Awards 2020 Winners and Nominees for Best Animated Feature Film

The Annie Awards are organized by the International Animated Film Society, ASIFA-Hollywood, and the 2020 Annie Awards, which was the 47th edition, celebrated animation achievements in 2019. Here are the nominees for the Best Animated Feature category at the 2020 Annie Awards:

1. "Frozen II" – Walt Disney Animation Studios
2. "How to Train Your Dragon: The Hidden World" – DreamWorks Animation
3. "Klaus" – Netflix Presents A Production of The Spa Studios and Atresmedia Cine
4. "Missing Link" – Laika, LLC
5. "Toy Story 4" – Pixar Animation Studios

The winner of the Best Animated Feature at the 2020 Annie Awards was "Klaus", produced by Netflix and The Spa Studios.
Oscar Awards 1996 Winners and Nominees for Best Animated Feature Film

The category for Best Animated Feature Film was not introduced at the Academy Awards until 2001. Therefore, there were no nominees or winners for Best Animated Feature Film at the 1996 Oscars, which recognized films from 1995.

The first film to receive the Academy Award for Best Animated Feature was "Shrek," in 2001. Prior to the introduction of this category, animated films could be nominated for other awards, including Best Picture, like "Beauty and the Beast" which was nominated in 1992. Animated films were typically recognized in categories such as Best Visual Effects or Best Original Song, among others.
Academy Awards 2002 Winners and Nominees for Best Animated Feature Film and Best Animated Short Film

The 74th Academy Awards held in 2002 honored films from 2001. This marked the first year the category for Best Animated Feature Film was introduced. Below are the nominees and winner for both Best Animated Feature and Best Animated Short Film:

**Best Animated Feature Film:**

1. "Shrek" – DreamWorks Animation (Winner)
2. "Jimmy Neutron: Boy Genius" – Nickelodeon Movies, O Entertainment, DNA Productions
3. "Monsters, Inc." – Pixar Animation Studios

"Shrek" was the inaugural winner of this new Oscar category.

**Best Animated Short Film:**

1. "For the Birds" – Pixar Animation Studios (Winner)
2. "Fifty Percent Grey" – Ruairi Robinson and Seamus Byrne
3. "Give Up Yer Aul Sins" – Brown Bag Films
4. "Strange Invaders" – Cordell Barker
5. "Stubble Trouble" – Joseph E. Merideth

"For the Birds" from Pixar won the award for Best Animated Short Film that year.
Academy Awards 2010 Winners and Nominees for Best Animated Feature Film and Best Animated Short Film

The 82nd Academy Awards, held in 2010, honored films released in 2009. Here are the nominees and winners for the categories of Best Animated Feature Film and Best Animated Short Film:

**Best Animated Feature Film:**

1. "Up" – Pixar Animation Studios (Winner)
2. "Coraline" – Laika
3. "Fantastic Mr. Fox" – American Empirical Pictures, Indian Paintbrush, Regency Enterprises
4. "The Princess and the Frog" – Walt Disney Animation Studios
5. "The Secret of Kells" – Cartoon Saloon

"Up" won the award for Best Animated Feature Film and was also notable for being only the second animated film in history to be nominated for Best Picture.

**Best Animated Short Film:**

1. "Logorama" – H5, Autour de Minuit Productions, Anarchy Post (Winner)
2. "Granny O'Grimm's Sleeping Beauty" – Brown Bag Films
3. "French Roast" – Pumpkin Factory/Bibo Films
4. "The Lady and the Reaper (La Dama y la Muerte)" – Kandor Graphics, Green Moon Espana
5. "A Matter of Loaf and Death" – Aardman Animations

"Logorama" won the Oscar for Best Animated Short Film.
Academy Awards 2011 Winners and Nominees for Best Animated Feature Film and Best Animated Short Film

The 83rd Academy Awards, held in 2011, recognized films from 2010. Here are the nominees and winners for Best Animated Feature Film and Best Animated Short Film:

**Best Animated Feature Film:**

1. "Toy Story 3" – Pixar Animation Studios (Winner)
2. "How to Train Your Dragon" – DreamWorks Animation
3. "The Illusionist" (L'Illusionniste) – Django Films

"Toy Story 3" was the winner of the Best Animated Feature Film category and was also nominated for Best Picture at this ceremony.

**Best Animated Short Film:**

1. "The Lost Thing" – Shaun Tan and Andrew Ruhemann (Winner)
2. "Day & Night" – Pixar Animation Studios
3. "The Gruffalo" – Magic Light Pictures
4. "Let's Pollute" – Geefwee Boedoe
5. "Madagascar, a Journey Diary (Madagascar, Carnet de Voyage)" – Bastien Dubois

"The Lost Thing" won the Oscar for Best Animated Short Film.
Academy Awards 2012 Winners and Nominees for Best Animated Feature Film and Best Animated Short Film

The 84th Academy Awards, held in 2012, honored films from 2011. Here are the nominees and the winner for the Best Animated Feature Film and Best Animated Short Film categories:

**Best Animated Feature Film:**

1. "Rango" – Paramount Pictures, Nickelodeon Movies, Gore Verbinski, Graham King, and John B. Carls (Winner)
2. "A Cat in Paris" (Une vie de chat) – Folimage
3. "Chico & Rita" – Magic Light Pictures
4. "Kung Fu Panda 2" – DreamWorks Animation
5. "Puss in Boots" – DreamWorks Animation

"Rango" won the Oscar for Best Animated Feature Film.

**Best Animated Short Film:**

1. "The Fantastic Flying Books of Mr. Morris Lessmore" – Moonbot Studios (Winner)
2. "Dimanche/Sunday" – ONF
3. "La Luna" – Pixar Animation Studios
4. "A Morning Stroll" – Studio AKA
5. "Wild Life" – National Film Board of Canada

"The Fantastic Flying Books of Mr. Morris Lessmore" was awarded the Oscar for Best Animated Short Film.
Academy Awards 2013 Winners and Nominees for Best Animated Feature Film and Best Animated Short Film

The 85th Academy Awards, held in 2013, celebrated the films of 2012. Below are the nominees and winner for the categories of Best Animated Feature Film and Best Animated Short Film:

**Best Animated Feature Film:**

1. "Brave" – Mark Andrews and Brenda Chapman (Winner)
2. "Frankenweenie" – Tim Burton
3. "ParaNorman" – Sam Fell and Chris Butler
4. "The Pirates! Band of Misfits" – Peter Lord
5. "Wreck-It Ralph" – Rich Moore

"Brave" won the award for Best Animated Feature Film.

**Best Animated Short Film:**

1. "Paperman" – John Kahrs (Winner)
2. "Adam and Dog" – Minkyu Lee
3. "Fresh Guacamole" – PES
4. "Head over Heels" – Timothy Reckart and Fodhla Cronin O'Reilly
5. "The Longest Daycare" – David Silverman

"Paperman" took home the Oscar for Best Animated Short Film.
Academy Awards 2019 Winners and Nominees for Best Animated Feature Film and Best Animated Short Film

The 91st Academy Awards, held in 2019, celebrated the films of 2018. Here are the nominees and winners for Best Animated Feature Film and Best Animated Short Film:

**Best Animated Feature Film:**

1. "Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse" – Bob Persichetti, Peter Ramsey, Rodney Rothman, Phil Lord, and Christopher Miller (Winner)
2. "Incredibles 2" – Brad Bird, John Walker, and Nicole Paradis Grindle
3. "Isle of Dogs" – Wes Anderson, Scott Rudin, Steven Rales, and Jeremy Dawson
4. "Mirai" – Mamoru Hosoda and Yuichiro Saito
5. "Ralph Breaks the Internet" – Rich Moore, Phil Johnston, and Clark Spencer

"Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse" won the Academy Award for Best Animated Feature Film.

**Best Animated Short Film:**

1. "Bao" – Domee Shi and <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON> (Winner)
2. "Animal Behaviour" – Alison Snowden and David Fine
3. "Late Afternoon" – Louise Bagnall and Nuria González Blanco
4. "One Small Step" – Andrew Chesworth and Bobby Pontillas
5. "Weekends" – Trevor Jimenez

"Bao" won the Oscar for Best Animated Short Film.
Academy Awards 2020 Winners and Nominees for Best Animated Feature Film and Best Animated Short Film

The 92nd Academy Awards, held in 2020, recognized films released in 2019. Below are the nominees and winners for the categories of Best Animated Feature Film and Best Animated Short Film:

**Best Animated Feature Film:**

1. "Toy Story 4" – Josh Cooley, Mark Nielsen, and Jonas Rivera (Winner)
2. "How to Train Your Dragon: The Hidden World" – Dean DeBlois, Bradford Lewis, and Bonnie Arnold
3. "I Lost My Body" – Jérémy Clapin and Marc du Pontavice
4. "Klaus" – Sergio Pablos, Jinko Gotoh, and Marisa Román
5. "Missing Link" – Chris Butler, Arianne Sutner, and Travis Knight

"Toy Story 4" won the Oscar for Best Animated Feature Film.

**Best Animated Short Film:**

1. "Hair Love" – Matthew A. Cherry and Karen Rupert Toliver (Winner)
2. "Dcera (Daughter)" – Daria Kashcheeva
3. "Kitbull" – Rosana Sullivan and Kathryn Hendrickson
4. "Memorable" – Bruno Collet and Jean-François Le Corre
5. "Sister" – Siqi Song

"Hair Love" was awarded the Oscar for Best Animated Short Film.
Academy Awards 2016  Winners and Nominees for Best Animated Feature Film and Best Animated Short Film

The 88th Academy Awards, held in 2016, celebrated the films of 2015. Below are the nominees and winners for the categories of Best Animated Feature Film and Best Animated Short Film:

**Best Animated Feature Film:**

1. "Inside Out" – Pete Docter and Jonas Rivera (Winner)
2. "Anomalisa" – Charlie Kaufman, Duke Johnson, and Rosa Tran
3. "Boy and the World" (O Menino e o Mundo) – Alê Abreu
4. "Shaun the Sheep Movie" – Mark Burton and Richard Starzak
5. "When Marnie Was There" (Omoide no Mānī) – Hiromasa Yonebayashi and <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>

"Inside Out" won the Oscar for Best Animated Feature Film.

**Best Animated Short Film:**

1. "Bear Story" (Historia de un oso) – Gabriel Osorio and Pato Escala (Winner)
2. "Prologue" – Richard Williams and Imogen Sutton
3. "Sanjay’s Super Team" – Sanjay Patel and Nicole Grindle
4. "We Can’t Live without Cosmos" (Мы не можем жить без космоса) – Konstantin Bronzit
5. "World of Tomorrow" – Don Hertzfeldt

"Bear Story" was awarded the Oscar for Best Animated Short Film.
Academy Awards 2016 Winners and Nominees for Best Animated Feature Film and Best Animated Short Film With the Special Guest of Character with here Come

The 88th Academy Awards, which took place in 2016, celebrated the achievements in film for the year 2015. Below are the winners and nominees in the Best Animated Feature Film and Best Animated Short Film categories. The request for a character and "with here come" part of the question seems to be a bit unclear, but I will provide the information on the winners and nominees as requested:

**Best Animated Feature Film:**

1. "Inside Out" – Directed by Pete Docter; Produced by Jonas Rivera (Winner)
2. "Anomalisa" – Directed by Charlie Kaufman and Duke Johnson; Produced by Rosa Tran
3. "Boy and the World" (O Menino e o Mundo) – Directed by Alê Abreu
4. "Shaun the Sheep Movie" – Directed by Mark Burton and Richard Starzak
5. "When Marnie Was There" (Omoide no Marnie) – Directed by Hiromasa Yonebayashi; Produced by Yoshiaki Nishimura

"Inside Out" won the Academy Award for Best Animated Feature Film.

**Best Animated Short Film:**

1. "Bear Story" (Historia de un Oso) – Directed by Gabriel Osorio; Produced by Pato Escala (Winner)
2. "Prologue" – Directed by Richard Williams; Producer: Imogen Sutton
3. "Sanjay's Super Team" – Directed by Sanjay Patel; Produced by Nicole Grindle
4. "We Can't Live without Cosmos" (Мы не можем жить без космоса) – Directed by Konstantin Bronzit
5. "World of Tomorrow" – Directed by Don Hertzfeldt

"Bear Story" won the Oscar for Best Animated Short Film.

Regarding the special guest or character aspect, often the Academy Awards invite notable characters or people related to the films to either present awards or for special appearances during the ceremony. If you're asking about a specific character that appeared during the 2016 Oscars, you might be referring to Woody and Buzz Lightyear from "Toy Story," as they are often associated with the animated categories due to their Pixar origins, especially considering "Inside Out" won that year. However, for detailed information about special segments or guests at that specific ceremony, it would be best to review the official Oscars footage or reports on the event.
Academy Awards 2016 Winners and Nominees for Best Animated Short Film With the Special Guest of Character with here Come

During the 88th Academy Awards held in 2016, the Oscar for Best Animated Short Film was awarded to "Bear Story," directed by Gabriel Osorio and produced by Pato Escala. Here are the nominees in that category:

1. "Bear Story" (Historia de un Oso) – Gabriel Osorio and Pato Escala (Winner)
2. "Prologue" – Richard Williams and Imogen Sutton
3. "Sanjay’s Super Team" – Sanjay Patel and Nicole Grindle
4. "We Can’t Live without Cosmos" (Мы не можем жить без космоса) – Konstantin Bronzit
5. "World of Tomorrow" – Don Hertzfeldt

As for special guests or characters, it is common for the Academy Awards to feature animated characters as part of the presentation for certain categories. For example, in past ceremonies, characters like Woody and Buzz Lightyear from the "Toy Story" franchise have made appearances. If there is a specific character or segment from the 2016 Oscars that you are referring to with "here Come," please provide additional details, and I will do my best to address your request.
